title: HANGEO Thursdays: Salsa & Bachata Night
description: Weekly Latin dance social with Salsa and Bachata. Beginner-friendly with instruction before the social dancing begins. Mingle, learn together, and enjoy a night of dance — a great way for couples to connect physically and have fun.

title: Comedy & Candles Rooftop Night: Love In The Dark
description: Candlelit rooftop comedy experience with craft cocktails and stand-up comedy. A uniquely romantic date night format — intimate vibes, good laughs, and Atlanta's skyline as your backdrop. Book early, this one fills up.

title: Lila Iké with Special Guests
location: The Loft at The Masquerade, 1374 West Peachtree St NW, Atlanta
description: Jamaican reggae star Lila Iké brings her captivating voice and modern reggae sound to Atlanta. One of the most talked-about reggae artists today — intimate venue, great energy, perfect for couples who love island music and good vibes.

title: 40th Annual Sweet Auburn Springfest
description: Atlanta's largest free multicultural outdoor festival celebrating its 40th anniversary. Live music, diverse food vendors, art, culture, and community. Great for couples who want a full day of exploration — multicultural, lively, and budget-friendly.

title: Helium Comedy Club: Martin Amini
location: Helium Comedy Club, 1157 Euclid Ave NE, Atlanta
description: Stand-up comedy with Martin Amini at Atlanta's premier comedy club. Laughter is a proven connection booster — a great date night choice. Multiple shows Saturday so you can pick an early or late slot.

title: Friday Jazz at the High Museum
location: High Museum of Art, 1280 Peachtree St NE, Atlanta
description: Live jazz in one of Atlanta's most iconic art museums. This month features Rafael Pereira and friends with two musical areas playing layered improv. Enjoy drinks, light bites, and docent-led gallery tours. Perfect date night — art, music, and atmosphere in one evening.

title: Audio Gumbo x Slow Drag
description: A monthly dance party celebrating Black intimacy and culture through 70s slow jams. Uplifting, romantic, and designed for connection. Ideal for couples who want a more soulful, intimate dancing experience rather than a club vibe.

title: AfroVibez 2026: 5th Annual Africa Day Celebration
description: Celebrate African heritage at this vibrant event. Dress code: Red, Yellow, or Green — colors representing unity, freedom, and pride. Great cultural date for couples interested in music, food, and African diaspora culture. High energy, great music, beautiful people.

title: Unbound Narratives: Embodied Language — Final Weekend
location: Atlanta Contemporary, 724 Peachtree St NE, Atlanta
description: Closing weekend for this powerful group exhibition exploring language, identity, and embodied experience. Featuring work by February James, Bethany Collins, Gabi Madrid, and a'driane nieves. Free admission — a sophisticated, low-cost date idea for art-loving couples.

title: Green Market at Piedmont Park
description: Atlanta's beloved weekly farmers market running through December. Over 50 vendors with fresh produce, artisan foods, plants, and more. Couples can spend a relaxing morning walking the park, tasting local foods, and picking up fresh ingredients together.

title: Reggae Fest Massive ATL
description: Major reggae festival featuring Mavado, Busy Signal, Beenie Man, Spice, Elephant Man, Ding Dong, and more. State Farm Arena — a big night out with incredible live performances. One of the premier reggae events of the year in Atlanta.

title: CARIBBEAN HEAT — Memorial Weekend Day Party
description: Island vibes day party for Memorial Weekend — Caribbean music, food, and good energy. Outdoor setting, great for couples who want a festive beach-party vibe without leaving Atlanta.
